extraction.one_line_summary: PHILIPSEN Jasper won his fourth sprint stage after a mistimed KRISTOFF Alexander lead-out hit the front too early, demonstrating superior finish reading.
tactical_patterns: UNO-X for KRISTOFF Alexander peaked 400m out allowing PHILIPSEN to time his afterburner perfectly with superior finish positioning.
Jumbo-Visma positioning VAN AERT Wout high in sprint finishes reveals dual mandate: protect VINGEGAARD Jonas while hunting stage wins.
URÁN Rigoberto actively helping VAN AERT in final positioning shows EF riders sacrificing GC support for stage win desperation.

rider_signals.PHILIPSEN Jasper: positive: Four from four on pure sprint stages reveals complete sprint dominance, never mistiming his effort regardless of lead-out quality.
rider_signals.KRISTOFF Alexander: negative: Lead-out peaked perfectly but legs failed to deliver in final 200m, suggesting form deficit not positioning error.
rider_signals.VAN AERT Wout: neutral: Ninth place with teammates and GC leader helping him position suggests team prioritizing his win chances over pure GC protection.
rider_signals.BAUHAUS Phil: negative: Failed to hold PHILIPSEN wheel despite perfect positioning, revealing raw speed deficit to the dominant sprinter.
